
### simplifying testing cases  {.smaller}


```c
int array[10];
void vulnerable(char *input) {
    char *p;
    int count = 0;
    p = input;
    if (*p == 'A') { 
        p += 1;
        while (*p == '0') {p += 1; count -= 1;}
        while (*p >= 'A' && *p < 'E') p += 1;
        while (*p == '0') {p += 1; count += 1;}
    }
    if (*p == 'B')
        array[count] += 1;
}

```
 

* example crash: [<code>A00ABDBBBDEEDDDCCCBBBDDDAAAA00000000000000000000000B</code>]{.my-small} 

   * might be what coverage-guided fuzzing finds

* would really prefer minimal example: <code>A00000000000B</code>


### automatically simplifying test cases  {.smaller}


* but look for <em>same result and/or coverage</em>
* systematic simplifications: 

   * try removing every character (one-by-one)
   * try decrementing every byte
   * …

* keep simplifications that don't change result
* AFL uses some of this strategy to help get better ‘base’ tests 

   * also has tool to do this on a found test
   * prefers simpler ‘base’ tests
